zotero.abstractNote: Deep learning models often rely on spurious correlations, or 'shortcuts', to make predictions, leading to poor generalization on out-of-distribution data. We propose a method to mitigate shortcut learning by generating counterfactual examples that preserve the target label while removing the shortcut features. We use a diffusion model to perform these counterfactual edits, and we train an ensemble of models on the original and counterfactual data. Our approach, which we call Diffusion-based Counterfactual Ensemble (DCE), significantly improves robustness to shortcut learning on several benchmarks, including synthetic datasets and real-world image classification tasks.
